package hub

import (
	"context"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
	"k8s.io/utils/clock"

	commonoptions "open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/common/options"
	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager"
	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/version"
)

// NewHubOperatorCmd generate a command to start hub operator
func NewHubOperatorCmd() *cobra.Command {
	opts := commonoptions.NewOptions()
	cmOptions := clustermanager.Options{}
	cmd := opts.
		NewControllerCommandConfig("clustermanager", version.Get(), cmOptions.RunClusterManagerOperator, clock.RealClock{}).
		NewCommandWithContext(context.TODO())
	cmd.Use = "hub"
	cmd.Short = "Start the cluster manager operator"

	flags := cmd.Flags()
	flags.BoolVar(&cmOptions.SkipRemoveCRDs, "skip-remove-crds", false, "Skip removing CRDs while ClusterManager is deleting.")
	flags.StringVar(&cmOptions.ControlPlaneNodeLabelSelector, "control-plane-node-label-selector",
		"node-role.kubernetes.io/master=", "control plane node labels, "+
			"e.g. 'environment=production', 'tier notin (frontend,backend)'")
	flags.Int32Var(&cmOptions.DeploymentReplicas, "deployment-replicas", 0,
		"Number of deployment replicas, operator will automatically determine replicas if not set")
	flags.BoolVar(&cmOptions.EnableSyncLabels, "enable-sync-labels", false,
		"If set, will sync the labels of ClusterManager CR to all hub resources")
	opts.AddFlags(flags)
	return cmd
}

func (o *Options) RunClusterManagerOperator(ctx context.Context, controllerContext *controllercmd.ControllerContext) error {
	// setting up contextual logger

	deploymentInformer := informers.NewSharedInformerFactoryWithOptions(kubeClient, 5*time.Minute,
		informers.WithTweakListOptions(func(options *metav1.ListOptions) {
			selector := &metav1.LabelSelector{
				MatchExpressions: []metav1.LabelSelectorRequirement{
					{
						Key:      helpers.HubLabelKey,
						Operator: metav1.LabelSelectorOpExists,
					},
				},
			}
			options.LabelSelector = metav1.FormatLabelSelector(selector)
		}))

	secretInformers := map[string]corev1informers.SecretInformer{
		helpers.SignerSecret:              signerSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
		helpers.RegistrationWebhookSecret: registrationSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
		helpers.WorkWebhookSecret:         workSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
		helpers.AddonWebhookSecret:        addonSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
		helpers.GRPCServerSecret:          grpcServerSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
	}

	if err != nil {

134		case *apiextensionsv1.CustomResourceDefinition:

func ApplyDirectly(
	ctx context.Context,
	client kubernetes.Interface,
	apiExtensionClient apiextensionsclient.Interface,
	recorder events.Recorder,
	cache resourceapply.ResourceCache,
	manifests resourceapply.AssetFunc,
	files ...string) []resourceapply.ApplyResult {
	var ret []resourceapply.ApplyResult
	recorderWrapper := commonrecorder.NewEventsRecorderWrapper(ctx, recorder)

	var genericApplyFiles []string
	for _, file := range files {
		result := resourceapply.ApplyResult{File: file}
		objBytes, err := manifests(file)
		if err != nil {
			result.Error = fmt.Errorf("missing %q: %v", file, err)
			ret = append(ret, result)
			continue
		}

		requiredObj, _, err := genericCodec.Decode(objBytes, nil, nil)
		if err != nil {
			result.Error = fmt.Errorf("cannot decode %q: %v", file, err)
			ret = append(ret, result)
			continue
		}

		// Special treatment on webhook, apiservices, and endpoints.
		result.Type = fmt.Sprintf("%T", requiredObj)
		switch t := requiredObj.(type) {
		case *admissionv1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration:
			result.Result, result.Changed, result.Error = ApplyValidatingWebhookConfiguration(
				client.AdmissionregistrationV1(), t)
		case *admissionv1.MutatingWebhookConfiguration:
			result.Result, result.Changed, result.Error = ApplyMutatingWebhookConfiguration(
				client.AdmissionregistrationV1(), t)
		case *corev1.Endpoints:
			result.Result, result.Changed, result.Error = ApplyEndpoints(context.TODO(), client.CoreV1(), t)
		default:
			genericApplyFiles = append(genericApplyFiles, file)
		}

		ret = append(ret, result)
	}
	clientHolder := resourceapply.NewKubeClientHolder(client).WithAPIExtensionsClient(apiExtensionClient)
	applyResults := resourceapply.ApplyDirectly(
		ctx,
		clientHolder,
		recorderWrapper,
		cache,
		manifests,
		genericApplyFiles...,
	)

	ret = append(ret, applyResults...)
	return ret
}

// NumOfUnavailablePod is to check if a deployment is in degraded state.

96	func CleanUpStaticObject(
97		ctx context.Context,
98		client kubernetes.Interface,
99		apiExtensionClient apiextensionsclient.Interface,
100		apiRegistrationClient apiregistrationclient.APIServicesGetter,
101		manifests resourceapply.AssetFunc,
102		file string) error {
103		objectRaw, err := manifests(file)
104		if err != nil {
105			return err
106		}
107		object, _, err := genericCodec.Decode(objectRaw, nil, nil)
108		if err != nil {
109			return err
110		}
111		switch t := object.(type) {
112		case *corev1.Namespace:
113			err = client.CoreV1().Namespaces().Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
114		case *appsv1.Deployment:
115			err = client.AppsV1().Deployments(t.Namespace).Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
116		case *corev1.Endpoints:
117			err = client.CoreV1().Endpoints(t.Namespace).Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
118		case *corev1.Service:
119			err = client.CoreV1().Services(t.Namespace).Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
120		case *corev1.ServiceAccount:
121			err = client.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts(t.Namespace).Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
122		case *corev1.ConfigMap:
123			err = client.CoreV1().ConfigMaps(t.Namespace).Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
124		case *corev1.Secret:
125			err = client.CoreV1().Secrets(t.Namespace).Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
126		case *rbacv1.ClusterRole:
127			err = client.RbacV1().ClusterRoles().Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
128		case *rbacv1.ClusterRoleBinding:
129			err = client.RbacV1().ClusterRoleBindings().Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
130		case *rbacv1.Role:
131			err = client.RbacV1().Roles(t.Namespace).Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
132		case *rbacv1.RoleBinding:
133			err = client.RbacV1().RoleBindings(t.Namespace).Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
134		case *apiextensionsv1.CustomResourceDefinition:
135			if apiExtensionClient == nil {
136				err = fmt.Errorf("apiExtensionClient is nil")
137			} else {
138				err = apiExtensionClient.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
139			}
140		case *apiregistrationv1.APIService:
141			if apiRegistrationClient == nil {
142				err = fmt.Errorf("apiRegistrationClient is nil")
143			} else {
144				err = apiRegistrationClient.APIServices().Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
145			}
146		case *admissionv1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration:
147			err = client.AdmissionregistrationV1().ValidatingWebhookConfigurations().Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
148		case *admissionv1.MutatingWebhookConfiguration:
149			err = client.AdmissionregistrationV1().MutatingWebhookConfigurations().Delete(ctx, t.Name, metav1.DeleteOptions{})
150		default:
151			err = fmt.Errorf("unhandled type %T", object)
152		}
153		if errors.IsNotFound(err) {
154			return nil
155		}
156		return err
157	}

159	func ApplyValidatingWebhookConfiguration(
160		client admissionclient.ValidatingWebhookConfigurationsGetter,
161		required *admissionv1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration) (*admissionv1.ValidatingWebhookConfiguration, bool, error) {
162		existing, err := client.ValidatingWebhookConfigurations().Get(context.TODO(), required.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
163		if errors.IsNotFound(err) {
164			actual, err := client.ValidatingWebhookConfigurations().Create(context.TODO(), required, metav1.CreateOptions{})
165			return actual, true, err
166		}
167		if err != nil {
168			return nil, false, err
169		}
170	
171		modified := resourcemerge.BoolPtr(false)
172		existingCopy := existing.DeepCopy()
173		resourcemerge.EnsureObjectMeta(modified, &existingCopy.ObjectMeta, required.ObjectMeta)
174		if !equality.Semantic.DeepEqual(existingCopy.Webhooks, required.Webhooks) {
175			*modified = true
176			existing.Webhooks = required.Webhooks
177		}
178		if !*modified {
179			return existing, false, nil
180		}
181		actual, err := client.ValidatingWebhookConfigurations().Update(context.TODO(), existing, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
182		return actual, true, err
183	}

98	func (c *hubReconcile) reconcile(ctx context.Context, cm *operatorapiv1.ClusterManager,
99		config manifests.HubConfig) (*operatorapiv1.ClusterManager, reconcileState, error) {
100		// If AddOnManager is not enabled, remove related resources
101		if !config.AddOnManagerEnabled {
102			_, _, err := cleanResources(ctx, c.hubKubeClient, cm, config, hubAddOnManagerRbacResourceFiles...)
103			if err != nil {
104				return cm, reconcileStop, err
105			}
106		}
107	
108		// Remove work-controller deployment if feature not enabled
109		if !config.WorkControllerEnabled {
110			_, _, err := cleanResources(ctx, c.hubKubeClient, cm, config, workControllerResourceFiles...)
111			if err != nil {
112				return cm, reconcileStop, err
113			}
114		}
115	
116		// Remove grpc server related resources if grpc auth is disabled
117		if !config.GRPCAuthEnabled {
118			_, _, err := cleanResources(ctx, c.hubKubeClient, cm, config, grpcServerResourceFiles...)
119			if err != nil {
120				return cm, reconcileStop, err
121			}
122		}
123	
124		hubResources := getHubResources(cm.Spec.DeployOption.Mode, config)
125		var appliedErrs []error
126	
127		resourceResults := helpers.ApplyDirectly(
128			ctx,
129			c.hubKubeClient,
130			nil,
131			c.recorder,
132			c.cache,
133			func(name string) ([]byte, error) {
134				template, err := manifests.ClusterManagerManifestFiles.ReadFile(name)
135				if err != nil {
136					return nil, err
137				}
138				objData := assets.MustCreateAssetFromTemplate(name, template, config).Data
139				helpers.SetRelatedResourcesStatusesWithObj(ctx, &cm.Status.RelatedResources, objData)
140				return objData, nil
141			},
142			hubResources...,
143		)
144		for _, result := range resourceResults {
145			if result.Error != nil {
146				appliedErrs = append(appliedErrs, fmt.Errorf("%q (%T): %v", result.File, result.Type, result.Error))
147			}
148		}
149	
150		if len(appliedErrs) > 0 {
151			meta.SetStatusCondition(&cm.Status.Conditions, metav1.Condition{
152				Type:    operatorapiv1.ConditionClusterManagerApplied,
153				Status:  metav1.ConditionFalse,
154				Reason:  "HubResourceApplyFailed",
155				Message: fmt.Sprintf("Failed to apply hub resources: %v", utilerrors.NewAggregate(appliedErrs)),
156			})
157			return cm, reconcileStop, utilerrors.NewAggregate(appliedErrs)
158		}
159	
160		return cm, reconcileContinue, nil
161	}
162	
163	func (c *hubReconcile) clean(ctx context.Context, cm *operatorapiv1.ClusterManager,
164		config manifests.HubConfig) (*operatorapiv1.ClusterManager, reconcileState, error) {
165		hubResources := getHubResources(cm.Spec.DeployOption.Mode, config)
166		return cleanResources(ctx, c.hubKubeClient, cm, config, hubResources...)
167	}
168	
169	func getHubResources(mode operatorapiv1.InstallMode, config manifests.HubConfig) []string {
170		hubResources := []string{namespaceResource}
171		hubResources = append(hubResources, hubRbacResourceFiles...)
172		if config.AddOnManagerEnabled {
173			hubResources = append(hubResources, hubAddOnManagerRbacResourceFiles...)
174		}
175	
176		if config.WorkControllerEnabled {
177			hubResources = append(hubResources, workControllerResourceFiles...)
178		}
179	
180		if config.GRPCAuthEnabled {
181			hubResources = append(hubResources, grpcServerResourceFiles...)
182		}
183	
184		// the hubHostedWebhookServiceFiles are only used in hosted mode
185		// Note: addon conversion webhook is not supported in hosted mode
186		if helpers.IsHosted(mode) {
187			hubResources = append(hubResources, hubHostedWebhookServiceFiles...)
188			if config.RegistrationWebhook.HostedIsIPFormat {
189				hubResources = append(hubResources, hubHostedWebhookEndpointRegistration)
190			}
191			if config.WorkWebhook.HostedIsIPFormat {
192				hubResources = append(hubResources, hubHostedWebhookEndpointWork)
193			}
194		} else {
195			hubResources = append(hubResources, hubDefaultWebhookServiceFiles...)
196		}
197	
198		return hubResources
199	}

45		AutoApprovedARNPatterns           string
46		AwsResourceTags                   string
47		Labels                            map[string]string
48		LabelsString                      string
49		GRPCAuthEnabled                   bool

51		GRPCAutoApprovedUsers             string

53		// TLS configuration injected into all managed hub component deployments

32			"If set, will sync the labels of ClusterManager CR to all hub resources")
33		opts.AddFlags(flags)

32		EnableSyncLabels              bool
33	}
34	
35	// RunClusterManagerOperator starts a new cluster manager operator
36	func (o *Options) RunClusterManagerOperator(ctx context.Context, controllerContext *controllercmd.ControllerContext) error {
37		// setting up contextual logger
38		logger := klog.NewKlogr()
39		podName := os.Getenv("POD_NAME")
40		if podName != "" {
41			logger = logger.WithValues("podName", podName)
42		}
43		ctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)
44	
45		// Build kubclient client and informer for managed cluster
46		kubeClient, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(controllerContext.KubeConfig)
47		if err != nil {
48			return err
49		}
50	
51		newOneTermInformer := func(name string) informers.SharedInformerFactory {
52			return informers.NewSharedInformerFactoryWithOptions(kubeClient, 5*time.Minute,
53				informers.WithTweakListOptions(func(options *metav1.ListOptions) {
54					options.FieldSelector = fields.OneTermEqualSelector("metadata.name", name).String()
55				}))
56		}
57	
58		signerSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.SignerSecret)
59		registrationSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.RegistrationWebhookSecret)
60		workSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.WorkWebhookSecret)
61		addonSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.AddonWebhookSecret)
62		grpcServerSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.GRPCServerSecret)
63		configmapInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.CaBundleConfigmap)
64	
65		deploymentInformer := informers.NewSharedInformerFactoryWithOptions(kubeClient, 5*time.Minute,
66			informers.WithTweakListOptions(func(options *metav1.ListOptions) {
67				selector := &metav1.LabelSelector{
68					MatchExpressions: []metav1.LabelSelectorRequirement{
69						{
70							Key:      helpers.HubLabelKey,
71							Operator: metav1.LabelSelectorOpExists,
72						},
73					},
74				}
75				options.LabelSelector = metav1.FormatLabelSelector(selector)
76			}))
77	
78		secretInformers := map[string]corev1informers.SecretInformer{
79			helpers.SignerSecret:              signerSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
80			helpers.RegistrationWebhookSecret: registrationSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
81			helpers.WorkWebhookSecret:         workSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
82			helpers.AddonWebhookSecret:        addonSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
83			helpers.GRPCServerSecret:          grpcServerSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
84		}
85	
86		// Build operator client and informer
87		operatorClient, err := operatorclient.NewForConfig(controllerContext.KubeConfig)
88		if err != nil {
89			return err
90		}
91		operatorInformer := operatorinformer.NewSharedInformerFactory(operatorClient, 5*time.Minute)
92	
93		// Load TLS config and start watching for changes. The watcher restarts the process
94		// when the ConfigMap changes so the new TLS settings are applied.
95		currentTLSConfig, err := tlslib.StartTLSConfigMapWatcher(ctx, kubeClient, controllerContext.OperatorNamespace,
96			func() {
97				logger.Info("TLS ConfigMap changed, restarting", "component", "cluster-manager operator")
98				os.Exit(0)
99			},
100		)
101		if err != nil {
102			if ctx.Err() != nil {
103				return nil
104			}
105			return err
106		}
107		tlsMinVersion := tlslib.VersionToString(currentTLSConfig.MinVersion)
108		tlsCipherSuites := tlslib.CipherSuitesToString(currentTLSConfig.CipherSuites)
109		logger.Info("TLS configuration loaded", "minVersion", tlsMinVersion, "cipherSuites", tlsCipherSuites)
110	
111		clusterManagerController := clustermanagercontroller.NewClusterManagerController(
112			kubeClient,
113			controllerContext.KubeConfig,
114			operatorClient.OperatorV1().ClusterManagers(),
115			operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers(),
116			deploymentInformer.Apps().V1().Deployments(),
117			configmapInformer.Core().V1().ConfigMaps(),
118			o.SkipRemoveCRDs,
119			o.ControlPlaneNodeLabelSelector,
120			o.DeploymentReplicas,
121			controllerContext.OperatorNamespace,
122			o.EnableSyncLabels,
123			tlsMinVersion,
124			tlsCipherSuites,
125		)
126	
127		statusController := clustermanagerstatuscontroller.NewClusterManagerStatusController(
128			operatorClient.OperatorV1().ClusterManagers(),
129			operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers(),
130			deploymentInformer.Apps().V1().Deployments())
131	
132		certRotationController := certrotationcontroller.NewCertRotationController(
133			kubeClient,
134			secretInformers,
135			configmapInformer.Core().V1().ConfigMaps(),
136			operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers())
137	
138		crdMigrationController := migrationcontroller.NewCRDMigrationController(
139			controllerContext.KubeConfig,
140			kubeClient,
141			operatorClient.OperatorV1().ClusterManagers(),
142			operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers())
143	
144		crdStatusController := crdstatuccontroller.NewCRDStatusController(
145			controllerContext.KubeConfig,
146			kubeClient,
147			operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers())
148	
149		go operatorInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
150		go deploymentInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
151		go signerSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
152		go registrationSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
153		go workSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
154		go addonSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
155		go grpcServerSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
156		go configmapInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
157		go clusterManagerController.Run(ctx, 1)
158		go statusController.Run(ctx, 1)
159		go certRotationController.Run(ctx, 1)
160		go crdMigrationController.Run(ctx, 1)
161		go crdStatusController.Run(ctx, 1)
162	
163		<-ctx.Done()
164		return nil
165	}

content.text:
**Summary of Module 2 changes across 6 files:**

1. **`manifests/config.go`** — Added `NetworkPolicyEnabled bool` to `HubConfig`
2. **`pkg/cmd/hub/operator.go`** — Registered `--enable-network-policy` flag (default: `false`)
3. **`pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go`** — Added `EnableNetworkPolicy bool` to `Options` struct, passed to controller constructor
4. **`pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_controller.go`** — Added `enableNetworkPolicy` field to controller struct, new constructor parameter, wired to `config.NetworkPolicyEnabled`
5. **`pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go`** — Added `networkPolicyFiles` variable, conditional apply in `getHubResources()`, cleanup when disabled in `reconcile()`
6. **`pkg/operator/helpers/helpers.go`** — Added `networkingv1` import and `*networkingv1.NetworkPolicy` case to `CleanUpStaticObject`

All changes follow the exact same pattern as existing `AddOnManagerEnabled`, `WorkControllerEnabled`, and `GRPCAuthEnabled` features.

52	// for cluster manager, it represents the hub cluster;
53	// while for klusterlet, it represents the managed cluster.

214	// ServiceAccountCtl watch service accounts and create a corresponding secret for it.
215	func ServiceAccountCtl(ctx context.Context, kubeClient kubernetes.Interface) {
216		w, err := kubeClient.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts("").Watch(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})
217		gomega.Expect(err).ToNot(gomega.HaveOccurred())
218		klog.Infof("service account controller start")
219	
220		for {
221			select {
222			case event, ok := <-w.ResultChan():
223				if !ok {
224					klog.Infof("channel closed, service account controller exit")
225					return
226				}
227	
228				sa, ok := event.Object.(*corev1.ServiceAccount)
229				if !ok {
230					klog.Infof("not a service account, continue")
231					continue
232				}
233	
234				if len(sa.Secrets) != 0 {
235					klog.Infof("service account secret already exist")
236					continue
237				}
238	
239				secret := &corev1.Secret{
240					ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
241						Name:      fmt.Sprintf("%s-token-test", sa.Name),
242						Namespace: sa.Namespace,
243						Annotations: map[string]string{
244							"kubernetes.io/service-account.name": sa.Name,
245						},
246					},
247					Data: map[string][]byte{
248						"token": []byte("test-token"),
249					},
250					Type: corev1.SecretTypeServiceAccountToken,
251				}
252	
253				_, err = kubeClient.CoreV1().Secrets(sa.Namespace).Create(ctx, secret, metav1.CreateOptions{})
254				if errors.IsAlreadyExists(err) {
255					klog.Infof("secret %s/%s already exist", secret.Namespace, secret.Name)
256				} else {
257					gomega.Expect(err).ToNot(gomega.HaveOccurred())
258				}
259	
260				_, err = kubeClient.CoreV1().Secrets(sa.Namespace).Get(ctx, secret.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
261				gomega.Expect(err).ToNot(gomega.HaveOccurred())
262	
263				err := retry.OnError(retry.DefaultBackoff,
264					func(e error) bool {
265						return true
266					},
267					func() error {
268						serviceAccount, err := kubeClient.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts(sa.Namespace).Get(ctx, sa.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
269						if err != nil {
270							return err
271						}
272						serviceAccount.Secrets = []corev1.ObjectReference{
273							{
274								Namespace: secret.Namespace,
275								Name:      secret.Name,
276							},
277						}
278						_, err = kubeClient.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts(serviceAccount.Namespace).Update(ctx, serviceAccount, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
279						return err
280					})
281				gomega.Expect(err).ToNot(gomega.HaveOccurred())
282			case <-ctx.Done():
283				klog.Infof("service account controller exit")
284				return
285			}
286		}
287	
288	}

package clustermanager

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"time"

	"github.com/openshift/library-go/pkg/controller/controllercmd"
	metav1 "k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1"
	"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields"
	"k8s.io/client-go/informers"
	corev1informers "k8s.io/client-go/informers/core/v1"
	"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes"
	"k8s.io/klog/v2"

	operatorclient "open-cluster-management.io/api/client/operator/clientset/versioned"
	operatorinformer "open-cluster-management.io/api/client/operator/informers/externalversions"
	tlslib "open-cluster-management.io/sdk-go/pkg/tls"

	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/operator/helpers"
	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/certrotationcontroller"
	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller"
	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/crdstatuccontroller"
	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/migrationcontroller"
	clustermanagerstatuscontroller "open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/statuscontroller"
)

type Options struct {
	SkipRemoveCRDs                bool
	ControlPlaneNodeLabelSelector string
	DeploymentReplicas            int32
	EnableSyncLabels              bool
	EnableNetworkPolicy           bool
}

// RunClusterManagerOperator starts a new cluster manager operator
func (o *Options) RunClusterManagerOperator(ctx context.Context, controllerContext *controllercmd.ControllerContext) error {
	// setting up contextual logger
	logger := klog.NewKlogr()
	podName := os.Getenv("POD_NAME")
	if podName != "" {
		logger = logger.WithValues("podName", podName)
	}
	ctx = klog.NewContext(ctx, logger)

	// Build kubclient client and informer for managed cluster
	kubeClient, err := kubernetes.NewForConfig(controllerContext.KubeConfig)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}

	newOneTermInformer := func(name string) informers.SharedInformerFactory {
		return informers.NewSharedInformerFactoryWithOptions(kubeClient, 5*time.Minute,
			informers.WithTweakListOptions(func(options *metav1.ListOptions) {
				options.FieldSelector = fields.OneTermEqualSelector("metadata.name", name).String()
			}))
	}

	signerSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.SignerSecret)
	registrationSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.RegistrationWebhookSecret)
	workSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.WorkWebhookSecret)
	addonSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.AddonWebhookSecret)
	grpcServerSecretInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.GRPCServerSecret)
	configmapInformer := newOneTermInformer(helpers.CaBundleConfigmap)

	deploymentInformer := informers.NewSharedInformerFactoryWithOptions(kubeClient, 5*time.Minute,
		informers.WithTweakListOptions(func(options *metav1.ListOptions) {
			selector := &metav1.LabelSelector{
				MatchExpressions: []metav1.LabelSelectorRequirement{
					{
						Key:      helpers.HubLabelKey,
						Operator: metav1.LabelSelectorOpExists,
					},
				},
			}
			options.LabelSelector = metav1.FormatLabelSelector(selector)
		}))

	secretInformers := map[string]corev1informers.SecretInformer{
		helpers.SignerSecret:              signerSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
		helpers.RegistrationWebhookSecret: registrationSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
		helpers.WorkWebhookSecret:         workSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
		helpers.AddonWebhookSecret:        addonSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
		helpers.GRPCServerSecret:          grpcServerSecretInformer.Core().V1().Secrets(),
	}

	// Build operator client and informer
	operatorClient, err := operatorclient.NewForConfig(controllerContext.KubeConfig)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	operatorInformer := operatorinformer.NewSharedInformerFactory(operatorClient, 5*time.Minute)

	// Load TLS config and start watching for changes. The watcher restarts the process
	// when the ConfigMap changes so the new TLS settings are applied.
	currentTLSConfig, err := tlslib.StartTLSConfigMapWatcher(ctx, kubeClient, controllerContext.OperatorNamespace,
		func() {
			logger.Info("TLS ConfigMap changed, restarting", "component", "cluster-manager operator")
			os.Exit(0)
		},
	)
	if err != nil {
		if ctx.Err() != nil {
			return nil
		}
		return err
	}
	tlsMinVersion := tlslib.VersionToString(currentTLSConfig.MinVersion)
	tlsCipherSuites := tlslib.CipherSuitesToString(currentTLSConfig.CipherSuites)
	logger.Info("TLS configuration loaded", "minVersion", tlsMinVersion, "cipherSuites", tlsCipherSuites)

	clusterManagerController := clustermanagercontroller.NewClusterManagerController(
		kubeClient,
		controllerContext.KubeConfig,
		operatorClient.OperatorV1().ClusterManagers(),
		operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers(),
		deploymentInformer.Apps().V1().Deployments(),
		configmapInformer.Core().V1().ConfigMaps(),
		o.SkipRemoveCRDs,
		o.ControlPlaneNodeLabelSelector,
		o.DeploymentReplicas,
		controllerContext.OperatorNamespace,
		o.EnableSyncLabels,
		o.EnableNetworkPolicy,
		tlsMinVersion,
		tlsCipherSuites,
	)

	statusController := clustermanagerstatuscontroller.NewClusterManagerStatusController(
		operatorClient.OperatorV1().ClusterManagers(),
		operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers(),
		deploymentInformer.Apps().V1().Deployments())

	certRotationController := certrotationcontroller.NewCertRotationController(
		kubeClient,
		secretInformers,
		configmapInformer.Core().V1().ConfigMaps(),
		operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers())

	crdMigrationController := migrationcontroller.NewCRDMigrationController(
		controllerContext.KubeConfig,
		kubeClient,
		operatorClient.OperatorV1().ClusterManagers(),
		operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers())

	crdStatusController := crdstatuccontroller.NewCRDStatusController(
		controllerContext.KubeConfig,
		kubeClient,
		operatorInformer.Operator().V1().ClusterManagers())

	go operatorInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
	go deploymentInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
	go signerSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
	go registrationSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
	go workSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
	go addonSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
	go grpcServerSecretInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
	go configmapInformer.Start(ctx.Done())
	go clusterManagerController.Run(ctx, 1)
	go statusController.Run(ctx, 1)
	go certRotationController.Run(ctx, 1)
	go crdMigrationController.Run(ctx, 1)
	go crdStatusController.Run(ctx, 1)

	<-ctx.Done()
	return nil
}

package hub

import (
	"context"

	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
	"k8s.io/utils/clock"

	commonoptions "open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/common/options"
	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager"
	"open-cluster-management.io/ocm/pkg/version"
)

// NewHubOperatorCmd generate a command to start hub operator
func NewHubOperatorCmd() *cobra.Command {
	opts := commonoptions.NewOptions()
	cmOptions := clustermanager.Options{}
	cmd := opts.
		NewControllerCommandConfig("clustermanager", version.Get(), cmOptions.RunClusterManagerOperator, clock.RealClock{}).
		NewCommandWithContext(context.TODO())
	cmd.Use = "hub"
	cmd.Short = "Start the cluster manager operator"

	flags := cmd.Flags()
	flags.BoolVar(&cmOptions.SkipRemoveCRDs, "skip-remove-crds", false, "Skip removing CRDs while ClusterManager is deleting.")
	flags.StringVar(&cmOptions.ControlPlaneNodeLabelSelector, "control-plane-node-label-selector",
		"node-role.kubernetes.io/master=", "control plane node labels, "+
			"e.g. 'environment=production', 'tier notin (frontend,backend)'")
	flags.Int32Var(&cmOptions.DeploymentReplicas, "deployment-replicas", 0,
		"Number of deployment replicas, operator will automatically determine replicas if not set")
	flags.BoolVar(&cmOptions.EnableSyncLabels, "enable-sync-labels", false,
		"If set, will sync the labels of ClusterManager CR to all hub resources")
	flags.BoolVar(&cmOptions.EnableNetworkPolicy, "enable-network-policy", false,
		"If set, will create NetworkPolicy resources in the hub namespace to restrict pod network access")
	opts.AddFlags(flags)
	return cmd
}

214	// ServiceAccountCtl watch service accounts and create a corresponding secret for it.
215	func ServiceAccountCtl(ctx context.Context, kubeClient kubernetes.Interface) {
216		w, err := kubeClient.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts("").Watch(ctx, metav1.ListOptions{})
217		gomega.Expect(err).ToNot(gomega.HaveOccurred())
218		klog.Infof("service account controller start")
219	
220		for {
221			select {
222			case event, ok := <-w.ResultChan():
223				if !ok {
224					klog.Infof("channel closed, service account controller exit")
225					return
226				}
227	
228				sa, ok := event.Object.(*corev1.ServiceAccount)
229				if !ok {
230					klog.Infof("not a service account, continue")
231					continue
232				}
233	
234				if len(sa.Secrets) != 0 {
235					klog.Infof("service account secret already exist")
236					continue
237				}
238	
239				secret := &corev1.Secret{
240					ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{
241						Name:      fmt.Sprintf("%s-token-test", sa.Name),
242						Namespace: sa.Namespace,
243						Annotations: map[string]string{
244							"kubernetes.io/service-account.name": sa.Name,
245						},
246					},
247					Data: map[string][]byte{
248						"token": []byte("test-token"),
249					},
250					Type: corev1.SecretTypeServiceAccountToken,
251				}
252	
253				_, err = kubeClient.CoreV1().Secrets(sa.Namespace).Create(ctx, secret, metav1.CreateOptions{})
254				if errors.IsAlreadyExists(err) {
255					klog.Infof("secret %s/%s already exist", secret.Namespace, secret.Name)
256				} else {
257					gomega.Expect(err).ToNot(gomega.HaveOccurred())
258				}
259	
260				_, err = kubeClient.CoreV1().Secrets(sa.Namespace).Get(ctx, secret.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
261				gomega.Expect(err).ToNot(gomega.HaveOccurred())
262	
263				err := retry.OnError(retry.DefaultBackoff,
264					func(e error) bool {
265						return true
266					},
267					func() error {
268						serviceAccount, err := kubeClient.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts(sa.Namespace).Get(ctx, sa.Name, metav1.GetOptions{})
269						if err != nil {
270							return err
271						}
272						serviceAccount.Secrets = []corev1.ObjectReference{
273							{
274								Namespace: secret.Namespace,
275								Name:      secret.Name,
276							},
277						}
278						_, err = kubeClient.CoreV1().ServiceAccounts(serviceAccount.Namespace).Update(ctx, serviceAccount, metav1.UpdateOptions{})
279						return err
280					})
281				gomega.Expect(err).ToNot(gomega.HaveOccurred())
282			case <-ctx.Done():
283				klog.Infof("service account controller exit")
284				return
285			}
286		}
287	
288	}

+1. Manifest files: default-deny-all.yaml, allow-apiserver-egress.yaml + NetworkPolicyEnabled in HubConfig
+2. Flag wiring: --enable-network-policy flag, conditional reconcile/cleanup in hub_reconcile.go, NetworkPolicy case in CleanUpStaticObject

content.text:
Module 3 (integration tests) is complete. Here's what was added:

**`test/integration/operator/clustermanager_test.go`**:
- Refactored `startHubOperator` to delegate to `startHubOperatorWithOptions`, which accepts a configuration callback — keeps existing callers unchanged while allowing new tests to customize options
- Added `ginkgo.Describe("ClusterManager NetworkPolicy")` block with two tests:
  - **"should create network policies when enabled"** — starts operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy = true`, verifies both `default-deny-all` and `allow-apiserver-egress` NetworkPolicies exist in `hubNamespace`
  - **"should not create network policies when disabled"** — starts operator without the flag, waits for namespace to exist, then uses `Consistently` to verify NetworkPolicies are NOT created

All 3 implementation modules are done. The workflow is now at the **verify** phase. Want me to run `make verify` and the relevant tests?
Running verification now.

Integration tests can't run locally — `etcd` is not installed (envtest requires kubebuilder assets with etcd and kube-apiserver binaries). This is an environment limitation, not a code issue. The test compiled and was recognized (2 of 60 specs matched the NetworkPolicy focus), it just can't start the envtest control plane.

Let me do a final summary of what passed and what didn't.

input.command:
git commit -s -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
:sparkles: Support NetworkPolicy for ClusterManager hub namespace

Add an --enable-network-policy flag to the cluster-manager operator that,
when set, creates two NetworkPolicy resources in the hub namespace:
- default-deny-all: denies all ingress and egress for hub pods
- allow-apiserver-egress: allows egress on DNS (UDP/TCP 53) and
  API server ports (TCP 443, 6443)

Policies target pods labeled with
open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager and are cleaned up
when the flag is disabled or the ClusterManager is deleted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.content:
1	# Design: Support policy network when creating namespace for ClusterManager
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	Add a `--enable-network-policy` flag to the ClusterManager operator binary. When enabled, two NetworkPolicy manifest files are applied to the `open-cluster-management-hub` namespace as part of the hub resource reconciliation. When disabled (default), the NetworkPolicies are cleaned up if they exist. This follows the same conditional resource pattern used by AddOnManager and WorkController.
6	
7	## Package & File Changes
8	
9	### manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/
10	
11	- `default-deny-all.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that denies all Ingress and Egress for pods with label `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager: {{ .ClusterManagerName }}`
12	- `allow-apiserver-egress.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that allows Egress on UDP/TCP ports 53, 443, 6443 for the same pod selector
13	
14	### manifests/
15	
16	- `config.go` — Add `NetworkPolicyEnabled bool` field to `HubConfig` struct
17	
18	### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/
19	
20	- `options.go` — Add `EnableNetworkPolicy bool` field to `Options` struct
21	
22	### pkg/cmd/hub/
23	
24	- `operator.go` — Register `--enable-network-policy` flag via `flags.BoolVar()`
25	
26	### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/
27	
28	- `clustermanager_controller.go` — Pass `EnableNetworkPolicy` option to `HubConfig.NetworkPolicyEnabled`
29	- `clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go` — Add `networkPolicyFiles` variable, conditionally include in `getHubResources()`, clean up when disabled
30	
31	### test/integration/operator/
32	
33	- `clustermanager_test.go` — Add test cases for NetworkPolicy creation when flag enabled and absence when disabled
34	
35	## Interface Definitions
36	
37	No new interfaces needed. Only struct field additions:
38	
39	```go
40	// In manifests/config.go - HubConfig struct
41	NetworkPolicyEnabled bool
42	
43	// In pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go - Options struct
44	EnableNetworkPolicy bool
45	```
46	
47	## Dependencies
48	
49	- Internal: `pkg/operator/helpers` (existing ApplyDirectly, CleanUpStaticObject), `manifests` (existing embed)
50	- External: none
51	- API repo: no changes needed
52	
53	## Implementation Modules
54	
55	1. **Manifest files + HubConfig** — Create NetworkPolicy YAML templates and add config field
56	   - Source files: `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/default-deny-all.yaml`, `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/allow-apiserver-egress.yaml`, `manifests/config.go`
57	   - No test file for this module (tested via integration)
58	   - Key scenarios: YAML renders correctly with template variables
59	
60	2. **Flag + conditional reconciliation** — Add binary flag, wire it through to hub reconcile, add conditional apply/cleanup logic
61	   - Source files: `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go`, `pkg/cmd/hub/operator.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_controller.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go`
62	   - No unit test file (logic is simple flag wiring, tested via integration)
63	   - Key scenarios: flag disabled = no NetworkPolicy; flag enabled = NetworkPolicies applied; flag disabled after enabled = cleanup
64	
65	3. **Integration tests** — Verify NetworkPolicy lifecycle with ClusterManager
66	   - Test file: `test/integration/operator/clustermanager_test.go`
67	   - Key scenarios:
68	     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=true`, verify both NetworkPolicies exist in hub namespace
69	     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=false` (default), verify no NetworkPolicies exist
70	     - Verify NetworkPolicies are cleaned up when ClusterManager is deleted
71	
72	## Test Strategy
73	
74	- Unit tests: none needed (no complex logic to unit test; flag wiring and manifest application are best tested via integration)
75	- Integration tests: `test/integration/operator/` suite
76	  - Add test case in `clustermanager_test.go` using existing `startHubOperator()` pattern with `EnableNetworkPolicy` set
77	  - Use `gomega.Eventually()` to check NetworkPolicy existence via `kubeClient.NetworkingV1().NetworkPolicies(hubNamespace).Get()`
78	  - Verify cleanup on deletion follows existing test patterns
79	
80	## Risks & Mitigations
81	
82	- **Pod selector mismatch**: The label value `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager` is set to `clusterManager.GetName()` (typically `"hub"`) at runtime. The manifest must use `{{ .ClusterManagerName }}` template variable, not a hardcoded value.
83	- **envtest NetworkPolicy support**: envtest may not enforce NetworkPolicies at the network level, but we can still verify the resources are created/deleted. Integration tests check resource existence, not network behavior.

file.content:
# Design: Support policy network when creating namespace for ClusterManager

## Overview

Add a `--enable-network-policy` flag to the ClusterManager operator binary. When enabled, two NetworkPolicy manifest files are applied to the `open-cluster-management-hub` namespace as part of the hub resource reconciliation. When disabled (default), the NetworkPolicies are cleaned up if they exist. This follows the same conditional resource pattern used by AddOnManager and WorkController.

## Package & File Changes

### manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/

- `default-deny-all.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that denies all Ingress and Egress for pods with label `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager: {{ .ClusterManagerName }}`
- `allow-apiserver-egress.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that allows Egress on UDP/TCP ports 53, 443, 6443 for the same pod selector

### manifests/

- `config.go` — Add `NetworkPolicyEnabled bool` field to `HubConfig` struct

### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/

- `options.go` — Add `EnableNetworkPolicy bool` field to `Options` struct

### pkg/cmd/hub/

- `operator.go` — Register `--enable-network-policy` flag via `flags.BoolVar()`

### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/

- `clustermanager_controller.go` — Pass `EnableNetworkPolicy` option to `HubConfig.NetworkPolicyEnabled`
- `clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go` — Add `networkPolicyFiles` variable, conditionally include in `getHubResources()`, clean up when disabled

### test/integration/operator/

- `clustermanager_test.go` — Add test cases for NetworkPolicy creation when flag enabled and absence when disabled

## Interface Definitions

No new interfaces needed. Only struct field additions:

```go
// In manifests/config.go - HubConfig struct
NetworkPolicyEnabled bool

// In pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go - Options struct
EnableNetworkPolicy bool
```

## Dependencies

- Internal: `pkg/operator/helpers` (existing ApplyDirectly, CleanUpStaticObject), `manifests` (existing embed)
- External: none
- API repo: no changes needed

## Implementation Modules

1. **Manifest files + HubConfig** — Create NetworkPolicy YAML templates and add config field
   - Source files: `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/default-deny-all.yaml`, `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/allow-apiserver-egress.yaml`, `manifests/config.go`
   - No test file for this module (tested via integration)
   - Key scenarios: YAML renders correctly with template variables

2. **Flag + conditional reconciliation** — Add binary flag, wire it through to hub reconcile, add conditional apply/cleanup logic
   - Source files: `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go`, `pkg/cmd/hub/operator.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_controller.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go`
   - No unit test file (logic is simple flag wiring, tested via integration)
   - Key scenarios: flag disabled = no NetworkPolicy; flag enabled = NetworkPolicies applied; flag disabled after enabled = cleanup

3. **Integration tests** — Verify NetworkPolicy lifecycle with ClusterManager
   - Test file: `test/integration/operator/clustermanager_test.go`
   - Key scenarios:
     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=true`, verify both NetworkPolicies exist in hub namespace
     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=false` (default), verify no NetworkPolicies exist
     - Verify NetworkPolicies are cleaned up when ClusterManager is deleted

## Test Strategy

- Unit tests: none needed (no complex logic to unit test; flag wiring and manifest application are best tested via integration)
- Integration tests: `test/integration/operator/` suite
  - Add test case in `clustermanager_test.go` using existing `startHubOperator()` pattern with `EnableNetworkPolicy` set
  - Use `gomega.Eventually()` to check NetworkPolicy existence via `kubeClient.NetworkingV1().NetworkPolicies(hubNamespace).Get()`
  - Verify cleanup on deletion follows existing test patterns

## Risks & Mitigations

- **Pod selector mismatch**: The label value `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager` is set to `clusterManager.GetName()` (typically `"hub"`) at runtime. The manifest must use `{{ .ClusterManagerName }}` template variable, not a hardcoded value.
- **envtest NetworkPolicy support**: envtest may not enforce NetworkPolicies at the network level, but we can still verify the resources are created/deleted. Integration tests check resource existence, not network behavior.

# Design: Support policy network when creating namespace for ClusterManager

## Overview

Add a `--enable-network-policy` flag to the ClusterManager operator binary. When enabled, two NetworkPolicy manifest files are applied to the `open-cluster-management-hub` namespace as part of the hub resource reconciliation. When disabled (default), the NetworkPolicies are cleaned up if they exist. This follows the same conditional resource pattern used by AddOnManager and WorkController.

## Package & File Changes

### manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/

- `default-deny-all.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that denies all Ingress and Egress for pods with label `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager: {{ .ClusterManagerName }}`
- `allow-apiserver-egress.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that allows Egress on UDP/TCP ports 53, 443, 6443 for the same pod selector

### manifests/

- `config.go` — Add `NetworkPolicyEnabled bool` field to `HubConfig` struct

### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/

- `options.go` — Add `EnableNetworkPolicy bool` field to `Options` struct

### pkg/cmd/hub/

- `operator.go` — Register `--enable-network-policy` flag via `flags.BoolVar()`

### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/

- `clustermanager_controller.go` — Pass `EnableNetworkPolicy` option to `HubConfig.NetworkPolicyEnabled`
- `clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go` — Add `networkPolicyFiles` variable, conditionally include in `getHubResources()`, clean up when disabled

### test/integration/operator/

- `clustermanager_test.go` — Add test cases for NetworkPolicy creation when flag enabled and absence when disabled

## Interface Definitions

No new interfaces needed. Only struct field additions:

```go
// In manifests/config.go - HubConfig struct
NetworkPolicyEnabled bool

// In pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go - Options struct
EnableNetworkPolicy bool
```

## Dependencies

- Internal: `pkg/operator/helpers` (existing ApplyDirectly, CleanUpStaticObject), `manifests` (existing embed)
- External: none
- API repo: no changes needed

## Implementation Modules

1. **Manifest files + HubConfig** — Create NetworkPolicy YAML templates and add config field
   - Source files: `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/default-deny-all.yaml`, `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/allow-apiserver-egress.yaml`, `manifests/config.go`
   - No test file for this module (tested via integration)
   - Key scenarios: YAML renders correctly with template variables

2. **Flag + conditional reconciliation** — Add binary flag, wire it through to hub reconcile, add conditional apply/cleanup logic
   - Source files: `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go`, `pkg/cmd/hub/operator.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_controller.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go`
   - No unit test file (logic is simple flag wiring, tested via integration)
   - Key scenarios: flag disabled = no NetworkPolicy; flag enabled = NetworkPolicies applied; flag disabled after enabled = cleanup

3. **Integration tests** — Verify NetworkPolicy lifecycle with ClusterManager
   - Test file: `test/integration/operator/clustermanager_test.go`
   - Key scenarios:
     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=true`, verify both NetworkPolicies exist in hub namespace
     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=false` (default), verify no NetworkPolicies exist
     - Verify NetworkPolicies are cleaned up when ClusterManager is deleted

## Test Strategy

- Unit tests: none needed (no complex logic to unit test; flag wiring and manifest application are best tested via integration)
- Integration tests: `test/integration/operator/` suite
  - Add test case in `clustermanager_test.go` using existing `startHubOperator()` pattern with `EnableNetworkPolicy` set
  - Use `gomega.Eventually()` to check NetworkPolicy existence via `kubeClient.NetworkingV1().NetworkPolicies(hubNamespace).Get()`
  - Verify cleanup on deletion follows existing test patterns

## Risks & Mitigations

- **Pod selector mismatch**: The label value `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager` is set to `clusterManager.GetName()` (typically `"hub"`) at runtime. The manifest must use `{{ .ClusterManagerName }}` template variable, not a hardcoded value.
- **envtest NetworkPolicy support**: envtest may not enforce NetworkPolicies at the network level, but we can still verify the resources are created/deleted. Integration tests check resource existence, not network behavior.

+- `chart/cluster-manager/templates/cluster_role.yaml` — Same networkpolicies rule for Helm chart
+- `olm-catalog/latest/manifests/cluster-manager.clusterserviceversion.yaml` — Same networkpolicies rule for OLM CSV
- `clustermanager_test.go` — Add test cases for NetworkPolicy creation when flag enabled and absence when disabled

Add a `--enable-network-policy` flag to the ClusterManager operator binary. When enabled, two NetworkPolicy manifest files are applied to the `open-cluster-management-hub` namespace as part of the hub resource reconciliation. When disabled (default), the NetworkPolicies are cleaned up if they exist. This follows the same conditional resource pattern used by AddOnManager and WorkController.

## Package & File Changes

### manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/

- `default-deny-all.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that denies all Ingress and Egress for pods with label `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager: {{ .ClusterManagerName }}`
- `allow-apiserver-egress.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that allows Egress on UDP/TCP ports 53, 443, 6443 for the same pod selector

### manifests/

- `config.go` — Add `NetworkPolicyEnabled bool` field to `HubConfig` struct

### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/

- `options.go` — Add `EnableNetworkPolicy bool` field to `Options` struct

### pkg/cmd/hub/

- `operator.go` — Register `--enable-network-policy` flag via `flags.BoolVar()`

### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/

- `clustermanager_controller.go` — Pass `EnableNetworkPolicy` option to `HubConfig.NetworkPolicyEnabled`
- `clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go` — Add `networkPolicyFiles` variable, conditionally include in `getHubResources()`, clean up when disabled

### deploy/cluster-manager/ (Operator RBAC)

- `config/rbac/cluster_role.yaml` — Add `networking.k8s.io` networkpolicies rule (get, list, create, update, patch, delete)
- `chart/cluster-manager/templates/cluster_role.yaml` — Same networkpolicies rule for Helm chart
- `olm-catalog/latest/manifests/cluster-manager.clusterserviceversion.yaml` — Same networkpolicies rule for OLM CSV

### test/integration/operator/

- `clustermanager_test.go` — Add test cases for NetworkPolicy creation when flag enabled and absence when disabled

## Interface Definitions

No new interfaces needed. Only struct field additions:

```go
// In manifests/config.go - HubConfig struct
NetworkPolicyEnabled bool

// In pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go - Options struct
EnableNetworkPolicy bool
```

## Dependencies

- Internal: `pkg/operator/helpers` (existing ApplyDirectly, CleanUpStaticObject), `manifests` (existing embed)
- External: none
- API repo: no changes needed

## Implementation Modules

1. **Manifest files + HubConfig** — Create NetworkPolicy YAML templates and add config field
   - Source files: `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/default-deny-all.yaml`, `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/allow-apiserver-egress.yaml`, `manifests/config.go`
   - No test file for this module (tested via integration)
   - Key scenarios: YAML renders correctly with template variables

2. **Flag + conditional reconciliation** — Add binary flag, wire it through to hub reconcile, add conditional apply/cleanup logic
   - Source files: `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go`, `pkg/cmd/hub/operator.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_controller.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go`
   - No unit test file (logic is simple flag wiring, tested via integration)
   - Key scenarios: flag disabled = no NetworkPolicy; flag enabled = NetworkPolicies applied; flag disabled after enabled = cleanup

3. **Integration tests** — Verify NetworkPolicy lifecycle with ClusterManager
   - Test file: `test/integration/operator/clustermanager_test.go`
   - Key scenarios:
     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=true`, verify both NetworkPolicies exist in hub namespace
     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=false` (default), verify no NetworkPolicies exist
     - Verify NetworkPolicies are cleaned up when ClusterManager is deleted

## Test Strategy

- Unit tests: none needed (no complex logic to unit test; flag wiring and manifest application are best tested via integration)
- Integration tests: `test/integration/operator/` suite
  - Add test case in `clustermanager_test.go` using existing `startHubOperator()` pattern with `EnableNetworkPolicy` set
  - Use `gomega.Eventually()` to check NetworkPolicy existence via `kubeClient.NetworkingV1().NetworkPolicies(hubNamespace).Get()`
  - Verify cleanup on deletion follows existing test patterns

## Risks & Mitigations

- **Pod selector mismatch**: The label value `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager` is set to `clusterManager.GetName()` (typically `"hub"`) at runtime. The manifest must use `{{ .ClusterManagerName }}` template variable, not a hardcoded value.
- **envtest NetworkPolicy support**: envtest may not enforce NetworkPolicies at the network level, but we can still verify the resources are created/deleted. Integration tests check resource existence, not network behavior.

# Design: Support policy network when creating namespace for ClusterManager

## Overview

Add a `--enable-network-policy` flag to the ClusterManager operator binary. When enabled, two NetworkPolicy manifest files are applied to the `open-cluster-management-hub` namespace as part of the hub resource reconciliation. When disabled (default), the NetworkPolicies are cleaned up if they exist. This follows the same conditional resource pattern used by AddOnManager and WorkController.

## Package & File Changes

### manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/

- `default-deny-all.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that denies all Ingress and Egress for pods with label `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager: {{ .ClusterManagerName }}`
- `allow-apiserver-egress.yaml` (NEW) — NetworkPolicy that allows Egress on UDP/TCP ports 53, 443, 6443 for the same pod selector

### manifests/

- `config.go` — Add `NetworkPolicyEnabled bool` field to `HubConfig` struct

### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/

- `options.go` — Add `EnableNetworkPolicy bool` field to `Options` struct

### pkg/cmd/hub/

- `operator.go` — Register `--enable-network-policy` flag via `flags.BoolVar()`

### pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/

- `clustermanager_controller.go` — Pass `EnableNetworkPolicy` option to `HubConfig.NetworkPolicyEnabled`
- `clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go` — Add `networkPolicyFiles` variable, conditionally include in `getHubResources()`, clean up when disabled

### deploy/cluster-manager/ (Operator RBAC)

- `config/rbac/cluster_role.yaml` — Add `networking.k8s.io` networkpolicies rule (get, list, create, update, patch, delete)
- `chart/cluster-manager/templates/cluster_role.yaml` — Same networkpolicies rule for Helm chart
- `olm-catalog/latest/manifests/cluster-manager.clusterserviceversion.yaml` — Same networkpolicies rule for OLM CSV

### test/integration/operator/

- `clustermanager_test.go` — Add test cases for NetworkPolicy creation when flag enabled and absence when disabled

## Interface Definitions

No new interfaces needed. Only struct field additions:

```go
// In manifests/config.go - HubConfig struct
NetworkPolicyEnabled bool

// In pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go - Options struct
EnableNetworkPolicy bool
```

## Dependencies

- Internal: `pkg/operator/helpers` (existing ApplyDirectly, CleanUpStaticObject), `manifests` (existing embed)
- External: none
- API repo: no changes needed

## Implementation Modules

1. **Manifest files + HubConfig** — Create NetworkPolicy YAML templates and add config field
   - Source files: `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/default-deny-all.yaml`, `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/allow-apiserver-egress.yaml`, `manifests/config.go`
   - No test file for this module (tested via integration)
   - Key scenarios: YAML renders correctly with template variables

2. **Flag + conditional reconciliation + operator RBAC** — Add binary flag, wire it through to hub reconcile, add conditional apply/cleanup logic, update operator ClusterRole with networking.k8s.io permissions
   - Source files: `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/options.go`, `pkg/cmd/hub/operator.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_controller.go`, `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/clustermanager_hub_reconcile.go`, `deploy/cluster-manager/config/rbac/cluster_role.yaml`, `deploy/cluster-manager/chart/cluster-manager/templates/cluster_role.yaml`, `deploy/cluster-manager/olm-catalog/latest/manifests/cluster-manager.clusterserviceversion.yaml`
   - No unit test file (logic is simple flag wiring, tested via integration)
   - Key scenarios: flag disabled = no NetworkPolicy; flag enabled = NetworkPolicies applied; flag disabled after enabled = cleanup

3. **Integration tests** — Verify NetworkPolicy lifecycle with ClusterManager
   - Test file: `test/integration/operator/clustermanager_test.go`
   - Key scenarios:
     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=true`, verify both NetworkPolicies exist in hub namespace
     - Start operator with `EnableNetworkPolicy=false` (default), verify no NetworkPolicies exist
     - Verify NetworkPolicies are cleaned up when ClusterManager is deleted

## Test Strategy

- Unit tests: none needed (no complex logic to unit test; flag wiring and manifest application are best tested via integration)
- Integration tests: `test/integration/operator/` suite
  - Add test case in `clustermanager_test.go` using existing `startHubOperator()` pattern with `EnableNetworkPolicy` set
  - Use `gomega.Eventually()` to check NetworkPolicy existence via `kubeClient.NetworkingV1().NetworkPolicies(hubNamespace).Get()`
  - Verify cleanup on deletion follows existing test patterns

## Risks & Mitigations

- **Pod selector mismatch**: The label value `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager` is set to `clusterManager.GetName()` (typically `"hub"`) at runtime. The manifest must use `{{ .ClusterManagerName }}` template variable, not a hardcoded value.
- **envtest NetworkPolicy support**: envtest may not enforce NetworkPolicies at the network level, but we can still verify the resources are created/deleted. Integration tests check resource existence, not network behavior.

131	  verbs: ["update", "patch"]

138	- apiGroups: ["register.open-cluster-management.io"]

144	- apiGroups: ["work.open-cluster-management.io"]

146	  verbs: ["update"]
147	- apiGroups: ["work.open-cluster-management.io"]
148	  resources: ["manifestworks"]

150	- apiGroups: ["work.open-cluster-management.io"]
151	  resources: ["manifestworks/status", "manifestworkreplicasets/status"]
152	  verbs: ["update", "patch"]
153	- apiGroups: ["flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io"]
154	  resources: ["flowschemas", "prioritylevelconfigurations"]
155	  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
156	- apiGroups: ["config.openshift.io"]
157	  resources: ["infrastructures"]
158	  verbs: ["get"]
159	- apiGroups: ["multicluster.x-k8s.io"]
160	  resources: ["clusterprofiles"]

162	- apiGroups: ["multicluster.x-k8s.io"]

164	  verbs: ["update", "patch"]
165	- apiGroups: [ "cluster.x-k8s.io" ]

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: cluster-manager
rules:
# Allow the registration-operator to create workload
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps", "namespaces", "serviceaccounts", "services"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete", "deletecollection"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["serviceaccounts/token"]
  verbs: ["create"]
# Allow gRPC client to connect to the gRPC server on hub
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["serviceaccounts/token"]
  verbs: ["subscribe"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["get"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "update", "patch", "delete"]
  resourceNames:
    - "signer-secret"
    - "registration-webhook-serving-cert"
    - "work-webhook-serving-cert"
    - "addon-webhook-serving-cert"
    - "registration-controller-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "registration-webhook-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "work-webhook-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "placement-controller-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "work-controller-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "addon-manager-controller-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "addon-webhook-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "external-hub-kubeconfig"
    - "work-driver-config"
    - "open-cluster-management-image-pull-credentials"
    - "grpc-server-serving-cert"
    - "cluster-import-config"
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets"]
  verbs: ["create"]
- apiGroups: ["coordination.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["leases"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["nodes"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["authorization.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["subjectaccessreviews"]
  verbs: ["create", "get"]
- apiGroups: ["", "events.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["events"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "patch", "update", "delete", "deletecollection"]
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["replicasets"]
  verbs: ["get"]  
- apiGroups: ["rbac.authorization.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["clusterrolebindings", "rolebindings"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["rbac.authorization.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["clusterroles", "roles"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete"]
# Allow the registration-operator to create crds
- apiGroups: ["apiextensions.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["customresourcedefinitions"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete"]
# Allow the registration-operator to update crds status
- apiGroups: ["apiextensions.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["customresourcedefinitions/status"]
  verbs: ["update", "patch"]
# Allow the registration-operator to create apiservice
- apiGroups: ["apiregistration.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["apiservices"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete"]
# Allow the registration-operator to create validatingwebhookconfigurration
- apiGroups: ["admissionregistration.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["validatingwebhookconfigurations", "mutatingwebhookconfigurations"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete"]
# Allow the nuclues to manage clustermanager apis.
- apiGroups: ["operator.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["clustermanagers"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "update", "delete", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["operator.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["clustermanagers/status"]
  verbs: ["update", "patch"]
# Allow the registration-operator to create storageversionmigration
- apiGroups: ["migration.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["storageversionmigrations"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete"]
# Some rbac needed in cluster-manager
- apiGroups: ["addon.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclusteraddons", "clustermanagementaddons"]
  verbs: ["create", "update", "patch", "get", "list", "watch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["addon.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclusteraddons/status", "clustermanagementaddons/status"]
  verbs: ["patch", "update"]
- apiGroups: ["addon.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: [managedclusteraddons/finalizers, "clustermanagementaddons/finalizers"]
  verbs: ["update"]
- apiGroups: ["addon.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: [addondeploymentconfigs, "addontemplates"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["authentication.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["tokenreviews"]
  verbs: ["create"]
- apiGroups: ["certificates.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["certificatesigningrequests"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["certificates.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["certificatesigningrequests/approval", "certificatesigningrequests/status"]
  verbs: ["update"]
- apiGroups: ["certificates.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["signers"]
  verbs: ["approve", "sign"]
# the grpc-sever requires the create permission for bootstrapping a managed cluster
- apiGroups: ["cluster.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclusters"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["cluster.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclustersetbindings", "placements", "addonplacementscores"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["cluster.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclustersets","placementdecisions"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["cluster.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclusters/status","managedclustersetbindings/status", "managedclustersets/status", "placements/status", "placementdecisions/status"]
  verbs: ["update", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["cluster.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["placements/finalizers"]
  verbs: ["update"]
- apiGroups: ["register.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclusters/clientcertificates"]
  verbs: ["renew"]
- apiGroups: ["register.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclusters/accept"]
  verbs: ["update"]
- apiGroups: ["work.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["manifestworkreplicasets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete", "deletecollection", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["work.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["manifestworkreplicasets/finalizers"]
  verbs: ["update"]
- apiGroups: ["work.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["manifestworks"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "delete", "deletecollection", "patch", "execute-as"]
- apiGroups: ["work.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["manifestworks/status", "manifestworkreplicasets/status"]
  verbs: ["update", "patch"]
- apiGroups: ["flowcontrol.apiserver.k8s.io"]
  resources: ["flowschemas", "prioritylevelconfigurations"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
- apiGroups: ["config.openshift.io"]
  resources: ["infrastructures"]
  verbs: ["get"]
- apiGroups: ["multicluster.x-k8s.io"]
  resources: ["clusterprofiles"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "create", "update", "patch", "delete"]
- apiGroups: ["multicluster.x-k8s.io"]
  resources: ["clusterprofiles/status"]
  verbs: ["update", "patch"]
- apiGroups: [ "cluster.x-k8s.io" ]
  resources: [ "clusters" ]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch"]
# for grpc-sever, the grpc-server need join permission for bootstrapping a managed cluster
- apiGroups: ["cluster.open-cluster-management.io"]
  resources: ["managedclustersets/join"]
  verbs: ["create"]

44	                },

58	    capabilities: Basic Install
59	    categories: Integration & Delivery,OpenShift Optional
60	    certified: "false"
61	    containerImage: quay.io/open-cluster-management/registration-operator:latest
62	    createdAt: "2026-04-01T09:36:58Z"
63	    description: Manages the installation and upgrade of the ClusterManager.
64	    operators.operatorframework.io/builder: operator-sdk-v1.32.0
65	    operators.operatorframework.io/project_layout: go.kubebuilder.io/v3
66	    repository: https://github.com/open-cluster-management-io/ocm
67	    support: Open Cluster Management Community
68	  name: cluster-manager.v9.9.9
69	  namespace: placeholder
70	spec:
71	  apiservicedefinitions: {}
72	  customresourcedefinitions:
73	    owned:
74	    - description: ClusterManager defines the configurations of controllers running
75	        hub that govern registration and work distribution for attached Klusterlets
76	      displayName: ClusterManager
77	      kind: ClusterManager
78	      name: clustermanagers.operator.open-cluster-management.io
79	      version: v1
80	  description: |
81	    The Cluster Manager provides the multicluster hub, which can manage Kubernetes-based clusters across data centers, public clouds, and private clouds. This operator supports the installation and upgrade of ClusterManager.
82	
83	    ## How to Install
84	    Install ClusterManager operator by following instructions in top right button `Install`. After installing the operator, create an instance of the ClusterManager resource to install the ClusterManager.
85	
86	    The pods of ClusterManager are created in the `open-cluster-management-hub` namespace:
87	
88	    ```
89	    $ kubectl get pods -n open-cluster-management-hub
90	    NAME                                                       READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
91	    cluster-manager-addon-manager-controller-7fcc67bfdf-x7hbz  1/1     Running   0          61m
92	    cluster-manager-placement-controller-dbcfd4975-26jpw       1/1     Running   0          61m
93	    cluster-manager-registration-controller-7949d97899-wwtg4   1/1     Running   0          61m
94	    cluster-manager-registration-webhook-7f675bc4bb-v4rhj      1/1     Running   0          61m
95	    cluster-manager-work-webhook-6c9d65fb95-jh97b              1/1     Running   0          61m
96	    ```
97	
98	    There is a ManagedCluster created in the Hub cluster when the managed cluster is registered to the Hub cluster.
99	
100	    ```
101	    $ kubectl get managedclusters.cluster.open-cluster-management.io
102	    NAME                   HUB ACCEPTED   MANAGED CLUSTER URLS     JOINED   AVAILABLE   AGE
103	    cluster-dev            true                                    True     True        7h20m
104	    cluster-test           false          https://127.0.0.1:8443                        7h25m
105	    ```

apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
kind: ClusterServiceVersion
metadata:
  annotations:
    alm-examples: |-
      [
        {
          "apiVersion": "operator.open-cluster-management.io/v1",
          "kind": "ClusterManager",
          "metadata": {
            "name": "cluster-manager"
          },
          "spec": {
            "addOnManagerImagePullSpec": "quay.io/open-cluster-management/addon-manager:latest",
            "deployOption": {
              "mode": "Default"
            },
            "placementImagePullSpec": "quay.io/open-cluster-management/placement:latest",
            "registrationConfiguration": {
              "featureGates": [
                {
                  "feature": "DefaultClusterSet",
                  "mode": "Enable"
                }
              ]
            },
            "registrationImagePullSpec": "quay.io/open-cluster-management/registration:latest",
            "workImagePullSpec": "quay.io/open-cluster-management/work:latest"
          }
        },
        {
          "apiVersion": "operator.open-cluster-management.io/v1",
          "kind": "ClusterManager",
          "metadata": {
            "name": "cluster-manager"
          },
          "spec": {
            "addOnManagerImagePullSpec": "quay.io/open-cluster-management/addon-manager:latest",
            "deployOption": {
              "hosted": {
                "registrationWebhookConfiguration": {
                  "address": "management-control-plane",
                  "port": 30443
                },
                "workWebhookConfiguration": {
                  "address": "management-control-plane",
                  "port": 31443
                }
              },
              "mode": "Hosted"
            },
            "placementImagePullSpec": "quay.io/open-cluster-management/placement:latest",
            "registrationImagePullSpec": "quay.io/open-cluster-management/registration:latest",
            "workImagePullSpec": "quay.io/open-cluster-management/work:latest"
          }
        }
      ]
    capabilities: Basic Install
    categories: Integration & Delivery,OpenShift Optional
    certified: "false"
    containerImage: quay.io/open-cluster-management/registration-operator:latest
    createdAt: "2026-04-01T09:36:58Z"
    description: Manages the installation and upgrade of the ClusterManager.
    operators.operatorframework.io/builder: operator-sdk-v1.32.0
    operators.operatorframework.io/project_layout: go.kubebuilder.io/v3
    repository: https://github.com/open-cluster-management-io/ocm
    support: Open Cluster Management Community
  name: cluster-manager.v9.9.9
  namespace: placeholder
spec:
  apiservicedefinitions: {}
  customresourcedefinitions:
    owned:
    - description: ClusterManager defines the configurations of controllers running
        hub that govern registration and work distribution for attached Klusterlets
      displayName: ClusterManager
      kind: ClusterManager
      name: clustermanagers.operator.open-cluster-management.io
      version: v1
  description: |
    The Cluster Manager provides the multicluster hub, which can manage Kubernetes-based clusters across data centers, public clouds, and private clouds. This operator supports the installation and upgrade of ClusterManager.

    ## How to Install
    Install ClusterManager operator by following instructions in top right button `Install`. After installing the operator, create an instance of the ClusterManager resource to install the ClusterManager.

    The pods of ClusterManager are created in the `open-cluster-management-hub` namespace:

    ```
    $ kubectl get pods -n open-cluster-management-hub
    NAME                                                       READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
    cluster-manager-addon-manager-controller-7fcc67bfdf-x7hbz  1/1     Running   0          61m
    cluster-manager-placement-controller-dbcfd4975-26jpw       1/1     Running   0          61m
    cluster-manager-registration-controller-7949d97899-wwtg4   1/1     Running   0          61m
    cluster-manager-registration-webhook-7f675bc4bb-v4rhj      1/1     Running   0          61m
    cluster-manager-work-webhook-6c9d65fb95-jh97b              1/1     Running   0          61m
    ```

    There is a ManagedCluster created in the Hub cluster when the managed cluster is registered to the Hub cluster.

    ```
    $ kubectl get managedclusters.cluster.open-cluster-management.io
    NAME                   HUB ACCEPTED   MANAGED CLUSTER URLS     JOINED   AVAILABLE   AGE
    cluster-dev            true                                    True     True        7h20m
    cluster-test           false          https://127.0.0.1:8443                        7h25m
    ```

  icon:
  - base64data: 
    mediatype: image/png

---
# Source: cluster-manager/templates/cluster_role.yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: cluster-manager
rules:
# Allow the registration-operator to create workload
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps", "namespaces", "serviceaccounts", "services"]
  verbs: ["create", "get", "list", "update", "watch", "patch", "delete", "deletecollection"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["serviceaccounts/token"]
  verbs: ["create"]
# Allow gRPC client to connect to the gRPC server on hub
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["serviceaccounts/token"]
  verbs: ["subscribe"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["pods"]
  verbs: ["get"]
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["secrets"]
  verbs: ["get", "list", "watch", "update", "patch", "delete"]
  resourceNames:
    - "signer-secret"
    - "registration-webhook-serving-cert"
    - "work-webhook-serving-cert"
    - "addon-webhook-serving-cert"
    - "registration-controller-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "registration-webhook-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "work-webhook-sa-kubeconfig"
    - "placement-controller-sa-kubeconfig"

input.command:
gh pr create --repo zhiweiyin318/ocm --base main --head dev-workflow --title ":sparkles: Support NetworkPolicy for ClusterManager hub namespace" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

- Add an `--enable-network-policy` flag to the ClusterManager operator that creates two NetworkPolicy resources in the hub namespace when enabled:
  - `default-deny-all`: denies all ingress and egress for hub pods
  - `allow-apiserver-egress`: allows egress on DNS (UDP/TCP 53) and API server ports (TCP 443, 6443)
- Policies target pods labeled `open-cluster-management.io/created-by-clustermanager` and are cleaned up when the flag is disabled or the ClusterManager is deleted
- Grant `networking.k8s.io/networkpolicies` RBAC permissions to the operator across all deployment methods (kustomize, Helm, OLM)

## Changes

- `manifests/cluster-manager/hub/network-policy/` — two new NetworkPolicy YAML templates
- `manifests/config.go` — `NetworkPolicyEnabled` field in `HubConfig`
- `pkg/cmd/hub/operator.go` — `--enable-network-policy` flag registration
- `pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/` — flag wiring through Options, controller, and hub reconciler
- `pkg/operator/helpers/helpers.go` — `NetworkPolicy` case in `CleanUpStaticObject`
- `deploy/cluster-manager/` — operator ClusterRole RBAC in kustomize, Helm chart, and OLM CSV
- `test/integration/operator/clustermanager_test.go` — integration tests for NetworkPolicy create/absent scenarios

## Test plan

- [x] `go build ./cmd/...` passes
- [x] `go vet` passes on all changed packages
- [x] `make verify-fmt-imports` passes
- [x] Unit tests pass (`pkg/operator/operators/clustermanager/controllers/clustermanagercontroller/`, `pkg/operator/helpers/`)
- [ ] Integration tests (`test/integration/operator/` with `ginkgo.focus="NetworkPolicy"`) — requires envtest with etcd/kube-apiserver binaries

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)" 2>&1
